steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the curviness of the Mississippi River and Neales River using the notes. The answer must state which river has a higher sinuosity ratio — the direct measure of curviness.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The high-sinuosity Neales River in Australia is curvier than the low-sinuosity Mississippi River in the United States.: states the Neales River has a greater sinuosity ratio than the Mississippi — this reverses the notes. ✗
  • Choice B — In both the Mississippi and the Neales rivers, curviness is determined by comparing stream length to valley length.: describes how sinuosity is calculated rather than comparing the two rivers. ✗
  • Choice C — By comparing the stream lengths of the Mississippi and Neales rivers to their valley lengths, one can calculate their curviness, or sinuosity.: makes a claim about river length without comparing sinuosity ratios directly. ✗
  • Choice D — The Mississippi River's ratio of stream length to valley length, also known as sinuosity, is greater than that of the Neales River.: states the Mississippi's sinuosity ratio is greater than the Neales River's, directly identifying which river is curvier in line with the notes. ✓
D is correct.
